question.value: Every station on the N railroad sells tickets to every other station. When it added
some new Stations, 46 additional sets of tickets had to be printed. How many is "some"? How many stations were there before?

answer.value: If y stations are added to x already existing stations each new station will require (x + y - 1) sets of tickets: for y new stations this is y(x + y - 1) sets. Each old station needs y sets so: y(x+y-1)+xy = 46, or y(2x + y - 1) = 46. Thus y must be a positive integer which is a factor of 46: it is 1, 2, 23, or 46. "Some new stations" cannot be 1 station, and 23 and 46 give a minus number of old stations, so y = 2 and x= 11.
